paper_id: Prince Sales et al. - 2018 - Ontological Analysis and Redesign of Risk Modeling in ArchiMate

models.model_label: Ontology of Risk (fragment of the Common Ontology of Value and Risk, in OntoUML)

evidence_quotes.quote: In this paper, we address some challenges in conceptualizing and modeling risk with a systematic ontology-based approach. We investigate the concept of risk and related notions and propose an ontology of risk as a semantic foundation for the representation of risks in Enterprise Architecture.
The ontology of risk [19] formally captures our conceptualization of risk following the assumptions discussed in the previous section.

This analysis, which was grounded on the well-founded Common Ontology of Value and Risk [19], allowed us to clarify the real-world semantics underlying the risk-related constructs of the overlay, as well as to unveil several ontological deficiencies in it.

goals.justification: The ontology is explicitly created to conceptualize and clarify the notion of risk and serve as a semantic foundation, matching the For Understanding signals 'conceptualize', 'clarify', and clarifying real-world semantics.

evidence_quotes.quote: We use the proposed risk ontology to perform an ontological analysis of ArchiMate's Risk and Security Overlay (RSO).
In the following section we describe the ontological analysis we conducted to assess the risk-related constructs of RSO, in light of the risk ontology we have just discussed.
The nature of these different concepts represented by the same construct can be unveiled by means of the risk ontology discussed in Section 3.

goals.justification: The ontology is used as the evaluation lens to analyze RSO and identify its shortcomings (Limitations L1-L8), matching For Problem-solving signals 'analyze', 'evaluate', and 'identify shortcomings'. The ontology itself is the analytic instrument, not an implementation artifact.

models.model_label: Redesigned ArchiMate Risk and Security Overlay (RSO) risk modeling fragment

evidence_quotes.quote: Building on the results of this analysis, we propose a well-founded redesign of the risk modeling aspects of the RSO.

In order to address the identified shortcomings, we now propose a redesign of the risk-related portion of the RSO, which also follows its original strategy of only using existing ArchiMate constructs.
We then addressed these deficiencies by redesigning the risk modeling fragment of the RSO, making it more precise and expressive.

goals.justification: The authors redesign the RSO itself (a modeling language fragment) by mapping ontological concepts to specific ArchiMate constructs/stereotypes (Table 3), directly modifying the artifact. This matches For Intervening as the model is used to build/redesign a concrete artifact (the revised RSO).

evidence_quotes.quote: The redesigned risk modeling fragment proposes a number of solutions for the representation of notions that were not present in the original RSO.
We consider this a benefit of this scheme, as it enables risk to be integrated into an overall motivational analysis, even if specific details of events are omitted.

goals.justification: Beyond producing the redesigned artifact, the redesigned scheme is framed as enabling analysis (motivational analysis of risk), supporting decision-making and analysis activities by stakeholders, which matches For Problem-solving.

evidence_quotes.quote: Although this model provides valuable information for stakeholders of the Coldhard Steel company, it leaves some relevant questions unanswered

uncertain_goals.reason: Reference to stakeholders is about the original RSO examples and used to motivate limitations rather than as a stated purpose of the authors' redesigned model; evidence does not clearly establish synchronous communication as the goal.
